def: adj. S. प्र and चण्ड to be hot, furious. Flaming; blazing; raging; fierce.
adj. fem. of prāṅć, prāk, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Turned towards the front, or towards the east; belonging to the front or to the east; eastern, easterly; former, prior, preceding, ancient, old; — an ancient; — s.f. Hedge, fence, wall
more: adj. S. प्राच eastern. Ancient See پراچينتا (1). prāchīn adhkār , n. m. Prescriptive right; prescription. prāchīn rīt , n. f. Old or immemorial custom ( qadīm rasm ).
adj. Old, of former times, ancient.
subs: s.m. The plant Flacourtia cataphracta, and its fruit
s.m. The sacrificial cord (worn over the right shoulder and passed under the left arm)
s.m. 'The eastern Jaka tree,' the plant Ӕgle marmelos
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit प्राचीन (prācī́na) (with the sense "old" from Classical Sanskrit).

def: s.f. A species of plant, Clypea hermandifolia; or Cissampelos hexandra.
s.f. = S پراچينتو प्राचीनत्व prāćīnatva, s.m. Oldness, antiquity.
s.m. = S پراچينتا प्राचीनता prāćīnatā, s.f. Oldness, antiquity.
s.m. Enclosure, hedge, fence, wall, rampart.
s.m. A species of Sanskr̤it metre.
s.m. Instigating, inciting; directing, enjoining, prescribing, ordering; an order; a rule, law; saying; sending.
s.f. Prickly night-shade, Solanum jacquini.
part. Impelled; incited; prescribed, commanded, directed; sent.
One who sells flour, meal, pulse, &c.; a grocer.
more: n. m. A grocer; one who sells flour, dāl , etc. W.; a pedlar. E.

def: adj. Much, many, abundant, enough ( = bahut); abounding in, filled with, replete with; frequent.
s.f. = S پرچرتو प्रचुरत्व praćuratva, s.m. An abounding; abundance, plenty.
s.m. = S پرچرتا प्रचुरता praćuratā, s.f. An abounding; abundance, plenty.

adj. & s.m. Situated in front; belonging to the East, living in the East, eastern, &c. ( = prāćīn, q.v.); — the inhabitants of the east; the eastern country, the country south or east of the river Sarasvatī (which flows from the north-east towards the south-west).
part. Fallen (from), deviated (from), strayed.
adj. & s.m. Giving; — giver.
s.f. A gift; also = pra-da, q.v. adj. & s.m. Giving; — giver.

r: pra-daććhin, pradaćhin, pardaćhin, pra-daććhinā, par-daćhinā, pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā
def: s.m. = H پردچهنا प्रदच्छिणा pra-daććhinā, vulg.par-daćhinā, s.f. See pra-dakshiṇ and pra-dakshiṇā.
more: s. m. Going round an object to which respect is intended with the right always towards it (a religious ceremony), circuit, circumambulation.

r: pra-daććhinā, par-daćhinā, pra-daććhin, pradaćhin, pardaćhin, pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā
def: s.f. = H پردچهن प्रदच्छिण pra-daććhin, pradaćhin,pardaćhin, s.m. See pra-dakshiṇ and pra-dakshiṇā.
more: s. f. Going round an object to which respect is intended with the right always towards it (a religious ceremony), circuit, circumambulation.

r: pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā, pari-kramā, t̤awāf, pradakshiṇ karnā, kā
def: s.m. = S پردکشنا प्रदक्षिणा pra-dakshiṇā, s.f. Going round (an object), turning the right side (towards any object), reverential salutation by circumambulation from left to right (so that the right side is towards the person or object saluted; syn. pari-kramā; t̤awāf); one who is at the right hand of another; an object on the right; — pradakshiṇ karnā (-kā), To pass on the right (of), to turn the right side (towards).

r: pra-dakshiṇā, pra-dakshiṇ, pari-kramā, t̤awāf, pradakshiṇ karnā, kā
def: s.f. = S پردکشن प्रदक्षिण pra-dakshiṇ, s.m. Going round (an object), turning the right side (towards any object), reverential salutation by circumambulation from left to right (so that the right side is towards the person or object saluted; syn. pari-kramā; t̤awāf); one who is at the right hand of another; an object on the right; — pradakshiṇ karnā (-kā), To pass on the right (of), to turn the right side (towards).
s.m. Circumambulation by starting from the left and coming round to the right (by way of reverence).
s.m. = pradar, q.v. s.m. Splitting, rending; a fracture, cleft; an arrow; a particular disease of women, a flooding, mœnorrhagia.
s.m. Giving, offering, presenting, bestowing, granting; surrendering, giving up; bestowing in marriage; gift, donation; marriage.
s.m. Splitting, rending; a fracture, cleft; an arrow; a particular disease of women, a flooding, mœnorrhagia.
adj. & s.m. Foreshowing, foretelling, presaging; showing, exhibiting, displaying; proclaiming, announcing; propounding, expounding, teaching, instructing; — prophet; teacher, expounder.
s.m. Foreshowing prophesying; pointing out, exhibiting manifesting, &c.; seeing, viewing.
s.f. Offering; present of ceremony, a fee.
part. Foreshown, prophesied; shown forth, shown, brought to light, pointed out, declared, exhibited, manifested.
s.m. One who gives or bestows, giver, bestower, donor.
s.m. One who gives or bestows, giver, bestower, donor.
s.m. Plaster, a thick or viscid ointment, unguent; applying a plaster, unction.
s.m. The span of the thumb and fore-finger; — place, country.
s.m. Place, spot, region; country, district, province, foreign country; — a short span measured from the tip of the thumb to that of the forefinger.
s.m. Gift, present, bribe; an offering.
s.f. The index finger, the forefinger; the corresponding toe.
adj. & s.m. = par-desī, q.v.s.v. para, par.
s.f. Name of one of the daughters of Daksha.

adj. & s.m. Chief, principal, leading, main, capital; pre-eminent, most eminent, most excellent, best; — a chief or principal thing, chief object, the most important thing; primary or original matter, the germ out of which all material appearances are evolved; the natural state of anything; matter; nature; the Supreme Spirit, the Supreme Deity, intellect, understanding; the first companion or attendant of a king, minister, counsellor, courtier, noble; leader, head, chief, president; head man of a village
subs: s.m. The chief element or ingredient of the body, semen virile
(pradhāna+ut˚), adj. Best of the eminent, eminent, illustrious.

def: s.f. = S پردهانتو प्रधानत्व pradhānatva, s.m. Pre-eminence, principalship, chiefship, presidentship, superiority, excellence.
s.m. = S پردهانتا प्रधानता pradhānatā, s.f. Pre-eminence, principalship, chiefship, presidentship, superiority, excellence.
s.m. Predominance, preponderance, ascendency, pre-eminence, superiority, supremacy.
s.m. Utter destruction, annihilation; loss.
adj. & s.m. Destroying, annihilating; — a destroyer.
s.f. Direction, quarter, region of the sky; an intermediate point of the compass, or half-quarter (as north-east, south-west, &c.).
s.m. A light, lamp, lantern; splendour, brilliance.
s.m. Kindling, inflaming, igniting, exciting, &c.; a sort of mineral poison (of a red colour and caustic in its action).
s.f. Direction, quarter, region of the sky; an intermediate point of the compass, or half-quarter (as north-east, south-west, &c.).
s.m. = pradosh, q.v. s.m. Defect, fault, offence, sin, transgression, guilt; the first part of the night, evening twilight, two mahūrtas after sunset.
s.m. Defect, fault, offence, sin, transgression, guilt; the first part of the night, evening twilight, two mahūrtas after sunset.
more: n. m. Evening; the first part of the night; two mahūrats after sunset.

def: s.m. The becoming manifest or visible, arising, coming into existence, appearance, manifestation; greatness, ascendency.
s.m. Judge, chief magistrate.
s.m. 'The pre-eminently mighty-one,' an epithet of Kām-dev the god of love (see parduman).
s.m. The ancient name of Allahabad
subs: s.m. A Hindū priest of Prāg.

def: used in comp. for prāk, q.v.
more: s. m. The ancient name of Ilāhābād.

def: adj. Bold, confident; resolute, energetic; prompt, ready; spirited, courageous, brave, intrepid, daring; audacious, proud, arrogant; strong, able; impudent, shameless; eminent.

s.f. = S پرگلبهتو प्रगल्भत्व pra-galbhatva, s.m. Boldness, confidence; energy, resoluteness, resolution; audacity, arrogance; power, eminence, consequence, prevalence; perverseness, wilfulness; impudence, shamelessness.

s.m. = S پرگلبهتا प्रगल्भता pra-galbhatā, s.f. Boldness, confidence; energy, resoluteness, resolution; audacity, arrogance; power, eminence, consequence, prevalence; perverseness, wilfulness; impudence, shamelessness.
s.m. Boldness, confidence; resoluteness, determination; pride, arrogance, effrontery; pomp, parade, rank; proficiency.
s.m. Proceeding; progress, advance.
adj. & s.m. Setting out, being about to depart; one who is about to depart.
adj. & s.m. Much, excessive; hard, difficult; hard, firm, steady; serious; — pain, privation, penance.
(S. pra-gāḍhatā), s.f. = H پرگاڙهتو प्रगाढ़त्व pra-gāṛhatva (S. pra-gāḍhatva), s.m. Abundance, excessiveness; hardness, difficulty; firmness, steadiness; seriousness.
(S. pra-gāḍhatva), s.m. = H پرگاڙهتا प्रगाढ़ता pra-gāṛhata (S. pra-gāḍhatā), s.f. Abundance, excessiveness; hardness, difficulty; firmness, steadiness; seriousness.
adj. = prakaṭ, q.v. adj. Evident, clear, manifest, apparent, obvious, visible; unfolded, displayed, open; public, issued out, commonly known, undisguised
more: adj. Obvious, notorious, publick, issued out, apparent, manifest, visible.
subs: v.t. To make evident, make known, to bring to light, divulge, disclose ( = prakāś karnā)
v.n. To become manifest; to appear, &c.

def: s.m. A singer.
= H پرگٿنا प्रगटना pragaṭnā, See prakaṭatā; and prakaṭnā.
= H پرگٿتا प्रगटता pragaṭatā, See prakaṭatā; and prakaṭnā.
more: v. n. To issue forth, to become manifest, to appear.

def: s.m. A singer.
adj. & s.m. Singing; — a singer.
s.m. = praghaṇ, q.v.
s.m. Covered terrace before the door of a house; porch, portico; a copper pot; an iron mace or crowbar; a species of bean, Phaseolus mungo.
adj. = pargaṭ; prakaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = prakaṭatā, q.v. s.f. = S پرکٿتو प्रकटत्व pra-kaṭaṭva, s.m. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
s.f. An iron trough into which silver or gold is cast previous to working it, a goldsmith's mould, &c.
s.f. = prakaṭatā, q.v. s.f. = S پرکٿتو प्रकटत्व pra-kaṭaṭva, s.m. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
more: s. f. 1. ( प्रघटी ) A mould in which silver or gold is cast previous to working it. 2. (corruption of प्रकटता ) Apparency, clearness.

def: s.m. Protection, preservation, salvation.
s.m. The highest point, summit
subs: adj. Taking the best share, taking the lead, going before; chief, principal.

def: s.m. Taking, seizing, holding, assuming; shutting up, confining; the commencement of an eclipse.
adj. & s.m. Chief, principal; — a chief.
s.f. Knowledge, understanding; a pandit's wife; a clever woman or girl.
adj. & s.m. Patient in investigation, wise, learned, sensible, clever; — a learned person, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Respecting the learned; — one who respects the learned.

def: s.f. Intelligence, understanding, wisdom, knowledge; a clever woman or girl (fem. of pragya)
adj. & s.m. Wise, intelligent, learned, sensible, clever; knowing, conversant (with); — a clever or sensible person.
adj. = pragya, q.v.; — s.m. Knowledge, wisdom, intelligence; mark, sign.
part. Known, understood; distinguished, discriminated, discerned; renowned, famous, notorious.
s.f. Knowledge; judiciousness, prudence.
(S. prājṅatā), s.f. = H پراگيتو प्राज्ञत्व prāgyatva, s.m. Learning, knowledge, understanding, wisdom, skill, cleverness.
s.m. = H پراگيتا प्राज्ञता prāgyatā (S. prājṅatā), s.f. Learning, knowledge, understanding, wisdom, skill, cleverness.
s.f. = prāgyā, q.v. s.f. Knowledge, understanding; a pandit's wife; a clever woman or girl.
s.m. Striking, hitting, pecking, wounding, killing; stroke, blow, thump, slap, knock, kick, shot, throw
more: s. m. The act of striking or beating, a blow, a stroke.

def: s.m. A watch, or three hours, &c., see pahar.
more: s. m. A watch of the day or night. See پـﮩر pahar.

def: adj. & s.m. Striking, smiting, beating, attacking, assailing, killing; — striker, smiter, fighter, warrior, hero; destroyer, murderer; debaser, humbler.
s.m. Striking, &c.; a desirable gift.
s.m. Striking, beating; pecking; biting; casting, throwing; attacking, assailing; — war, battle; a covered car, litter; the box of a carriage
subs: s.f. A species of the sarkarī metre.

def: adj. & s.m. = prahārak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Striking, smiting, beating, attacking, assailing, killing; — striker, smiter, fighter, warrior, hero; destroyer, murderer; debaser, humbler.
more: s. m. A striker, smiter, destroyer, debaser, humbler.

def: s.m. 'One who announces the hours by striking a bell, beating a gong, &c.'; a bellman, watchman, sentry, sentinel, patrol ( = pahriyā, q.v.).
part. Knocked; shot off, discharged (as a missile).
s.f. Watchfulness, vigilance; the office of a watchman or sentry.
v.t. To strike, &c. = prahār karnā, q.v.s.v. prahār. s.m. Striking, hitting, pecking, wounding, killing; stroke, blow, thump, slap, knock, kick, shot, throw
s.m. Extreme joy, hilarity, mirth, gladness, delight, rapture, exultation.
s.f. A species of metre.
part. Greatly pleased, overjoyed, rejoiced, delighted, very happy, merry, glad.
s.m. Loud laughter; hearty laugh; ridicule, derision; satire, irony; an actor, dancer; a name of Śiva.
s.m. Laughing loudly, violent or hearty laughter, mirth, merriment; laughing at, mocking, deriding; ridicule, irony, mockery; sarcasm, satire.
s.f. Arabian jasmine.
adj. Laughing heartily, laughing.
adj. Laughing aloud; causing laughter, diverting, joking, jesting; satirical.
s.f. A puzzling question, enigma, riddle (see pahelī).
s.m. Pleasure, joy, happiness; name of a pious Daitya, son of Hiraṇya-kaśipu, and regent of one of the divisions of Pātāl.
adj. & s.m. Causing joy or pleasure, rejoicing, refreshing; — one who causes joy, &c.
part. Rejoiced, delighted, joyous, happy.
part. = praharshit, q.v. part. Greatly pleased, overjoyed, rejoiced, delighted, very happy, merry, glad.
primary school
s.f. Progeny, offspring, brood, children, posterity, descendants, race, family; creature, created being; a subject, dependent, tenant, renter; people, subjects
more: n. f. S. प्रजा, Pālī. pajā . A subject; tenant. 1. Jaisā rājā, vaisī parjā . Prov. As the king is, so are his subjects. 2. Rājā rāj, parjā sukhī . Prov. When the king rules, his subjects prosper. 3. Parjā maran, rājā hāṅsī . Prov. The people die, the king holds high festivity!
s. f. A subject, tenant, renter, people.
subs: s.m. 'Protector of created beings,' an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa; protector of subjects, a king, sovereign
s.m. The protection of subjects or dependants

s.m. 'Lord of creatures,' an epithet of the deity, Brahmā; an epithet of a number of other divine personages; — ʻlord of the people,' a king, sovereign, prince; a daughter's husband, son-in-law; creator, procreator, father
s.m. The duty of children or of subjects; filial obedience; loyalty
(prajā+āśan), s.m. The food of subjects; — adj. & s.m. Eating up his subjects; — one who devours his subjects, a cruel and oppressive ruler, tyrant
adj. Killing offspring, destroying progeny ( = prajā-han)
s.m. Subjects, dependants, people, peasantry, ryots
s.f. A brother's wife; the wife of an elder brother; a mother, matron
adj. & s.m. Favourable to, or good for, children or subjects; kind or useful to children or subjects; — one who is kind to his dependants, &c.
adj. Destroying offspring; slaying one's subjects
(prajā+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of created beings,' an epithet of the god presiding over the procreation of offspring; 'lord of the people,' a prince, king, sovereign.

def: s.m. One who wakes; a watcher, guardian; the act of waking, or watching, or guarding.
s. m. Lord of subjects; a prince; a name of Brahmā.
s.m. = prajā-pati, q.v.
s.f. The giving away the whole of one's property before entering on the life of an ascetic or mendicant.

adj. & s.m. Belonging to, or relating to, or derived from Prajā-pati; — a form of marriage in which the bride is presented respectfully to the bridegroom by her father; a particular festival on the eighth day of the second half of the month Paush; a particular sacrifice performed before appointing a daughter to raise issue in default of male heirs; a kind of penance (which consists in eating but once a day, in the morning, for three consecutive days, then once in the night for three nights, then subsisting for three days on food given as alms, and then fasting three days more); the confluence of the Ganges and Jamna, Prayāg or Allahabad; the asterism Rohinī.

v.t. To burn, &c. = jalānā, q.v. v.t. (caus. of jalnā), To burn; to light, kindle; to fire; to inflame; to excite envy or jealousy; to vex, to irritate, enrage, exasperate; to grieve, to cause anguish to, to consume with sorrow; to smart, pain
subs: adj. & s.m. (Med.) Irritant; caustic; — an irritant.

def: s.f. A woman who has borne a child.
s.f. A mother.
s.m. Livelihood, subsistence.
s.f. See prāgyā.
adj. (f. -ā), Wise, intelligent, learned, knowing.
s.f. A clever or sensible woman; intelligence, understanding, intellect, wisdom, knowledge; discernment, discrimination, judgment.
s.m. Blazing up, flaming, burning, taking fire, kindling.
part. Flaming, burning, blazing; shining, bright, radiant.
s.m. = pra-jwār, q.v.
s.m. The heat of fever.
adj. & adv. Turning eastward, eastern, easterly; preceding in time or place, before, in front, previous, former, prior (in comp., before certain letters, prāk becomes prāg)
subs: s.m. Previous non-existence of anything, the non-existence of anything which may yet be, antecedent privation; non-existence (of an effect) previous (to production); (in Law) the non-possession of property that may be possessed
s.m. Prior or previous existence; superiority, excellence
s.f. The eleventh Nakshatra or lunar mansion
s.m. Name of the country Kāmrūp in Assam (the scene of Brahmā's penance)
s.m. Former age or time, previous time
adj. Belonging to former or ancient times, pertaining to a previous time, anterior, ancient, previous, former
adj. & s.m. Going before, preceding; — a precursor.

def: s.m. Trembling, shivering, quaking, violent motion
s.m. Causing to tremble; great trembling, violent motion; wind, air; name of a hell.
adj. Trembling, shaking, moving to and fro ( = prakampa-mān).
s.m. The stem or trunk of a tree; a branch, shoot; anything excellent of its kind; excellence; happiness.
s.m. Enclosure, fence, surrounding wall, rampart.
s.m. Heap, multitude, quantity, plenty; aloe-wood, Agallochum; — (rt. kr̤i), Aid, assistance, friendship; usage, custom.
s.m. Kind, sort, species; way, mode, manner, method, fashion; similitude; difference; speciality
more: n. m. 1. Make; structure; nature. 2. Kind; sort; class; division. Nānā prakār ke bhojan banāë . 3. Manner; method; way; mode. 4. Wealth; property; money ( dhan ). Apnā parkār rahait, taṅnenā kā janau kai lita hūṅ . Tir. Had I money I would celebrate the child's aneu . kaī prakār se . In many respects. kaī prakār ke, nānā parkār ke . Of many sorts.
s. m. Manner, method, kind, sort.
subs: (˚ra+an˚), s.m. Another method or sort; — adv. In another way
adj. (in Gram.) Expressive of kind, quality, or manner.

def: s.m. Treatment, discussion, expounding, explanation; subject, topic, province, department; section, chapter, book, paragraph; introduction, prologue, prelude; opportunity, occasion; event, circumstance, occurrence, affair; relation; a poetical fiction or poem (in which the story and principal persons are wholly imaginary); a place of pausing or of stopping.
more: s. m. Topick, subject; paragraph, section.

def: s.f. A drama of the same character as the prakaraṇ, but of less extent.
s.f. An episodical incident or interlude inserted in a drama to explain that which follows; a short interlude in a drama; a kind of song.
s.m. Pre-eminence, excellence, distinction, superiority; intensity (of good qualities, merit, &c.); protractedness, length; (in Gram.) the effect of the prefix pra upon roots.

adj. & s.m. Visible, manifest, clear, evident; open, public, conspicuous; noted, renowned, famous, notorious; illumined, bright, shining; — clearness, brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; light, daylight, sunshine; display, pomp, grandeur; manifestation, appearance, revelation, publicity, disclosure, elucidation; expansion, diffusion; — adv. Openly, publicly, manifestly, aloud
more: Illit. parkās ; Rus. pargās , n. m. S. प्र and काश shine. 1. Light; daylight; sunshine. 2. Splendour; pomp; magnificence; grandeur; glory. 3. Manifestation; appearance; display; revelation. prakāsh karnā , v. a. To make known; make public; manifest; declare; advertise. 2. To indicate; point out; tell.
subs: v.n. To obtain publicity, to come to light, to transpire, to obtain notoriety
(-kā), To make known, to disclose, reveal, declare; — prakāś karnā (-ko), To illumine, make manifest, display; to indicate, point out, make clear
adj. Becoming manifest, appearing, bright, brilliant, splendid, radiant
To be manifest; to be illumined, be elucidated

def: s.m. See pra-kāś.
more: 1. s. m. Light, daylight, splendour, display, manifestation, revelation, appearance. 2. adj. Apparent, manifest, conspicuous, clear, notorious, illumined.

def: adj. & s.m. Clear, bright, shining, brilliant, enlightened; illuminating, luminous; making apparent or manifest, disclosing, discovering, publishing; explaining, elucidating; — ʻthe giver of light,' the sun; an illustrator, expounder; publisher; discoverer.
s.f. = S پرکاشکتو प्रकाशकत्व pra-kāśakatva, s.m. The property of displaying or manifesting; luminousness, radiance ( = pra-kāśatā).
s.m. = S پرکاشکتا प्रकाशकता pra-kāśakatā, s.f. The property of displaying or manifesting; luminousness, radiance ( = pra-kāśatā).
s.m. Illuminating, giving light; making known, making clear or manifest, bringing to light; showing, pointing out.
s.f. = S پرکاشتو प्रकाशत्व pra-kāśatva, s.m. Brightness, brilliance, splendour, luminousness; appearance, manifestation, visibility; celebrity, renown, fame.
s.m. = S پرکاشتا प्रकाशता pra-kāśatā, s.f. Brightness, brilliance, splendour, luminousness; appearance, manifestation, visibility; celebrity, renown, fame.

adj. & s.m. = pra-kāśak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Clear, bright, shining, brilliant, enlightened; illuminating, luminous; making apparent or manifest, disclosing, discovering, publishing; explaining, elucidating; — ʻthe giver of light,' the sun; an illustrator, expounder; publisher; discoverer.
s. m. A discoverer.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Become visible, brought to light, visible, manifest, apparent, evident; displayed, unfolded, discovered; illumined, enlightened, elucidated; published, promulgated.
v.t. To illumine, &c., see pra-kāśan; and prakāś karnā, s.v. prakāś.
s.m. Clearness, brightness; celebrity, fame, renown.
part. adj. To be illuminated or enlightened; to be brought to light, to be made manifest; — s.m. Clearness, &c. = prā-kāśya, q.v.
adj. Evident, clear, manifest, apparent, obvious, visible; unfolded, displayed, open; public, issued out, commonly known, undisguised
subs: v.t. To make evident, make known, to bring to light, divulge, disclose ( = prakāś karnā)
v.n. To become manifest; to appear, &c.

def: s.f. = S پرکٿتو प्रकटत्व pra-kaṭaṭva, s.m. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
s.m. = S پرکٿتا प्रकटता pra-kaṭatā, s.f. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
part. Made visible or manifest, manifested, brought to light, unfolded, displayed.
part. Manifested, evident, apparent; unfolded, displayed, opened, expanded; publicly proclaimed or exhibited.
v.n. = prakaṭ honā, q.v.s.v. pra-kaṭ. adj. Evident, clear, manifest, apparent, obvious, visible; unfolded, displayed, open; public, issued out, commonly known, undisguised
subs: v.t. To make evident, make known, to bring to light, divulge, disclose ( = prakāś karnā)
v.n. To become manifest; to appear, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Very hot or acrid, biting, pungent; very hard or rough; — iron armour, &c. = pākhar, q.v.
s.m. Perception, the being known; publicity, &c. ( = prakhyāti, q.v.).
part. adj. Celebrated, renowned, famous, noted, notorious; pleased, happy.
s.f. Perceptibility; publicity; notoriety, celebrity, fame; praise, eulogium.

part. adj. & s.m. Scattered, strewed; spread abroad, spread, diffused, published, promulgated; — a confused mass; a miscellany; chapter, section, division of a book; a ćaurī or fly-flap, &c. = pra-kīrṇak, q.v.

adj. Scattered about, &c. = pra-kīrṇ, q.v.; — s.m. A ćaurī, the tail of the Bos gruniens used as a fan or fly-flap and as an ornament for horses; a section or division of a book; (in Law) decision, decree.
s.m. Announcing, proclaiming; pronouncing aloud; praising aloud, lauding, extolling.
s.f. Celebration; praise; fame, celebrity; declaration, mention.
part. Announced, proclaimed; pronounced, declared, mentioned, stated; called, named; explained; revealed; renowned, celebrated.
s.m. Effervescence, ebullition; excitement, emotion, violent anger, wrath, rage, fury; irritation, provocation.
s.m. Exciting, irritating, provoking.
part. Irritated, provoked, incensed, enraged.
s.m. Step, stride, pace; striding forwards, stepping; proceeding, going; commencement, beginning; paragraph, section (syn. dafʻa); leisure, opportunity; relation, proportion.
part. adj. Drawn forth, drawn out, protracted, lengthy; pre-eminent, superior, distinguished, excellent, exalted; prominent, chief, principal.
s.f. = S پرکرشٿتو प्रकृष्टत्व pra-kr̤ishṭatva, s.m. Transcendent excellence, pre-eminence, superiority.
s.m. = S پرکرشٿتا प्रकृष्टता pra-kr̤ishṭatā, s.f. Transcendent excellence, pre-eminence, superiority.

adj. Original, natural, not artificial, essential, unchanged, unaltered, unmodified; normal, ordinary, usual, common, uncultivated, unrefined, provincial, plebeian, vulgar; vernacular; — s.f. Any provincial or vernacular dialect akin to Sanskrit (especially spoken by the female characters and inferior personages of plays)
more: n. f. S. प्र before, कृ to make. lit . original. The vulgar Aryan vernacular of the Hindūs co-existent with the Sanskrit or literary language.
subs: s.m. The total dissolution of the world
s.m. Common or usual fever (occurring from affections of the wind in the rainy season, of the bile in the autumn, and of the phlegm in the spring)
s.m. The poets who wrote in Prākr̤it

def: part. Made, accomplished, completed; original; genuine, real, true, just, right, accurate, proper
more: s. f. Nature, disposition, property, quality.
subs: (˚ta+ar˚), adj. Having the original sense; real, true.

def: s. f. A dialect of the same origin as the Sanskrit language.
s.f. = S پرکرتتو प्रकृतत्व pra-kr̤itatva, s.m. Inchoate state or condition; genuineness, realness, truth, accuracy, propriety.
s.m. = S پرکرتتا प्रकृतता pra-kr̤itatā, s.f. Inchoate state or condition; genuineness, realness, truth, accuracy, propriety.
s.f. = prakr̤iti, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. Nature; the natural state or disposition. 2. Habit ( bān 2, 3). 1. Āi kāl hi bābu ka prākriti bigaṛi gilainih achhi, sadi khān gārī paṛhai chhathī . Tir. Father is out of sorts in these days, day and night he abuses every body. 2. Ek bastu gun hot haiṅ bhinn, prākritī ke bhāë, Bhanṭā ek ko pit hare, kare ek ko bāë . Sabhā bilās. The same thing acts in different ways on different men, Brinjal drives bile from some, makes wind in other men. 3. Jehane ekrā bāpakā prakrittī, tahane ekar prakrittī chhaik . Tir. He has his father's disposition.

def: s.f. The original or natural form (of anything), natural condition or state, original, primary substance; cause, original source; origin, extraction, descent; nature, character, constitution, disposition, habit, temper, temperament; rule, scheme, paradigm, pattern, model, standard; (in philosophy) the evolver of all material appearances, the originant or original source of the material world; (in Myth.) a goddess, the personified will of the Supreme in the creation (identified with Māyā or Illusion, and in an especial manner the prototype of the female sex); (in Gram.) the crude or elementary form of a word, an uninflected word; (in Arith.) a coefficient, multiplier; (in Anat.) temperament, the predominance of one of the humours at the time of generation; — the constituent elements of a State; a king's ministers; the subjects of a king, citizens, artizans, &c. (In some systems of Hindū philosophy prakr̤iti is considered the same with the Supreme Being)
subs: adj. Naturally changeful, volatile, fickle; dissolute, voluptuous
vulg. prakr̤iti-gyān, s.m. The knowledge of nature, &c. (see prakr̤iti)
adj. Being in the original or natural state, natural, genuine, unmixed, unadulterated; healthy, in good health; inherent, innate; bare, stripped of everything.

def: s.f. Manner, way; rite, observance; (in Gram.) rules for the inflection of words; elevation, dignity; bearing royal insignia.
s.m. Washing off, cleansing, cleaning, purifying; bathing; the expiation of a crime.
part. Washed, cleaned; purified, expiated.
s.m. Throwing, casting forth or forward, projecting; throw, cast.
s.m. Throwing, casting forth or forward, projecting; throw, cast.
part. Decayed, wasting.
part. Thrown, cast, flung.
s.m. Agitating, exciting.
adj. Former, prior, previous, anterior, antecedent, preceding; early, old, ancient; relating to a former state of existence, resulting from acts done in a former life
subs: s.m. An act formerly done, an act done in a former state of existence; fate, destiny.

def: part. Moved, agitated; very angry, enraged, incensed.
Hin. n. f. S. प्रापब्ध, H. lābh gain. Fate ( qismat ); predestination; destiny; fortune. Kāṅ Kāshī, kāṅ Kāshmīr, kāṅ Khurāsāṅ, Gujrāt, Tulsī, yahāṅ to jīv ko parālabadh le jāt . Tulsī Dās.
more: s. f. Fortune, fate, lot, destiny, predestination, venture, chance.

def: s.f. Fortune, fate, &c. = prārabdh, q.v. part. adj. Begun, commenced; caused by fate; — s.m. Beginning ( = prārabdhi); an undertaking.
s.m. Garland hanging down from the neck to the breast; necklace.

adj. & s.m. Hanging down, depending, pendulous; slow, dilatory; — procrastination, delay; new shoot or bud of a creeping plant; a branch; a garland of flowers worn round the neck; a necklace; — name of a demon killed by Balarām.
s.m. Talk, conversation, discourse; childish talk, prattle, prate, chattering; incoherent or delirious speech, raving; lamentation, wailing.
adj. & s.m. Talking much, chattering; talking incoherently or unmeaningly, or deliriously; — a prattler, &c.
s.m. A part cut off, chip, fragment (as of a reed, &c.); sheath (of a leaf).

s.m.f. (?). Dissolution, destruction, annihilation; the destruction of the whole world at the end of a kalpa, the consummation of all things; general destruction (such as at the flood or resurrection); any extensive destruction or devastation; death, dying; fainting, syncope, loss of sense or consciousness; (met.) vexation, fatigue, oppression, languor, disgust
more: s. f. 1. A general destruction, such as at the flood or resurrection. 2. ( met. ) Vexation, fatigue, oppression, languor, disgust.
subs: s.m. The period of the destruction of the world at the end of a kalpa, the time of universal destruction.

def: s.m. Unguent, ointment, plaster, salve.
adj. Anointing, smearing, plastering.
s.m. The act of anointing or smearing.
s.m. Allurement, seduction; desire, covetousness, cupidity, greediness.
s.m. Alluring, seducing, inducing, attracting.
adj. Alluring, seducing, attracting; inducing; lusting after, desiring, desirous, covetous, avaricious, greedy.
s.m. Rolling on the ground; heaving, tossing (as of the ocean).
adj. Rolling; heaving, tossing.
s.f. True perception, correct notion, accurate conception, true and certain knowledge, knowledge exempt from all error; consciousness; authority, example ( = pramāṇ).
more: s. (dakh.) Pox, gleet, clap ( Harris ): see پرميو parmeʼo.

def: s.m. Drunkenness, intoxication; madness, insanity, distraction, confusion, distress; stupidity; negligence, inattention, carelessness; inadvertence, error, inaccuracy, blunder, mistake.
s.f. A young and wanton woman; a handsome or beautiful woman; name of a Sanskrit metre.
more: n. f. S. प्रमदा A beautiful woman.

def: adj. & s.m. Intoxicated; insane; negligent, inattentive, inadvertent, careless, heedless, incautious, indifferent; — a careless person, &c.
adj. & s.m. = pramādī, and pramād, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Intoxicated; insane; negligent, inattentive, inadvertent, careless, heedless, incautious, indifferent; — a careless person, &c.
s.m. Madness, frenzy, fury; intoxication; the tree Justicia adhenatoda.
part. Immersed, dipped, drowned.

s.m. Measure, scale, standard (whether of weight, length, or capacity); magnitude, extent, length, weight, quantity, proportion, detail; rule, sanction, ground of assurance, standard, authority, judgment, proof, verification, attestation, warrant, precedent, guide, rule of conduct; instance, example; testimony, evidence; trust, belief, reliance; a scripture, a work of sacred authority; cause, motive; — adj. Authentic, authoritative, actual, substantial, real; approved of, admissible, credible, acceptable; — postp. Like, of the measure or standard (of, -ke), &c.
more: n. m. S. मा to measure. 1. Measure; scale; standard; magnitude; quantity; proportion. 2. Examination; trial; test; attestation; verification. 3. Proof; demonstration; testimony; authority; authoritative or scriptural assertion. 4. ( Law .). Proof by ordeal or oath; by evidence oral or documentary, and by possession. 5. Trust; belief; reliance. pramān, pramānī , adj. 1. Real; substantial; authentic; authoritative. 2. Approved; admissible; credible.

1. s. m. Authority, proof, verification, attestation, limit, instance, example, measure, quantity, detail. 2. adj. Authentick, actual, substantial, real, approved of, admissible, agreeable, acceptable. پرمان چڙهنا parmān chaṛhnā, To rise to a high rank.
subs: (˚na+abh˚), s.m. Absence of proof, want of authority
s.m. A written warrant; affidavit; voucher
s.m. 'A man who is an authority,' an umpire, arbitrator, judge
v.n. To rise to a high rank
v.n. To give evidence, to witness
s.f. Proof (or attempted proof) by appeal to records or to books (as distinguished from hearsay or tradition)
(-ko, or -kā), To assent (to), accept, credit, believe; to give evidence, to witness.

def: adj. = pramāṇ, q.v. s.m. Measure, scale, standard (whether of weight, length, or capacity); magnitude, extent, length, weight, quantity, proportion, detail; rule, sanction, ground of assurance, standard, authority, judgment, proof, verification, attestation, warrant, precedent, guide, rule of conduct; instance, example; testimony, evidence; trust, belief, reliance; a scripture, a work of sacred authority; cause, motive; — adj. Authentic, authoritative, actual, substantial, real; approved of, admissible, credible, acceptable; — postp. Like, of the measure or standard (of, -ke), &c.
subs: (˚na+abh˚), s.m. Absence of proof, want of authority
s.m. A written warrant; affidavit; voucher
s.m. 'A man who is an authority,' an umpire, arbitrator, judge
v.n. To rise to a high rank
v.n. To give evidence, to witness
s.f. Proof (or attempted proof) by appeal to records or to books (as distinguished from hearsay or tradition)
(-ko, or -kā), To assent (to), accept, credit, believe; to give evidence, to witness.

def: s.f. Rule, standard, authority; a kind of metre
subs: s.m. Establishing or admitting as authority, regarding or accepting as proof.

def: adj. & s.m. Established by proof, resting or founded on an authority, authoritative; genuine, authentic, credible, true; — one who rests his arguments on authority; a learned man; a president, the chief or head of a trade.

adj. & s.m. = prāmānik, q.v. adj. & s.m. Established by proof, resting or founded on an authority, authoritative; genuine, authentic, credible, true; — one who rests his arguments on authority; a learned man; a president, the chief or head of a trade.
more: see پرمان) 1. adj. Proper, creditable. 2. Reverend. 3. s. m. Chairman of an assembly, (in Bengal) a chief of a tribe among the inferiour casts.

def: s.f. A species of the Anushṭubh metre.
s.m. The being established by proof, the resting upon authority; the being an authority, or a rule, or proof; proof, evidence, authority, authenticity, credibility, genuineness.
s.m. = prāmāṇya, q.v. s.m. The being established by proof, the resting upon authority; the being an authority, or a rule, or proof; proof, evidence, authority, authenticity, credibility, genuineness.
adj. & s.m. Knowing truly or well, having a right notion or idea; — one who knows well, one who is competent to judge, an authority.
s.m. Maternal great-grandfather; — pra-mātāmahī, s.f. Maternal great-grandmother.
s.m. Name of a class of fiends attending on Śiva; one of that class; — a horse
subs: (˚tha+adh˚), s.m. 'Lord of the pramaths,' an epithet of Śiva, and also of Gaṇeś

def: s.f. The plant Terminalia chebula or citrina, and its fruit; yellow myrobalan.
adj. & s.m. Harassing, tormenting, torturing, paining, hurting, injuring, killing, slaughter; — tormentor, killer, &c.
part. adj. Excited, wanton, lascivious; drunken, &c. ( = pra-mādī, q.v.); intoxicated with delight, enraptured.
s. m. A gonorrhœa, a (venereal) clap, a gleet.
s.m. Urinary disease; gonorrhœa, clap, gleet.
part. adj. Meted out, measured; known, understood; established by argument, demonstrated, proved; authentic
subs: (˚ta+ak˚), s.f. 'Having measured syllables,' a species of the jagatī metre.

def: s.f. Measure, measurement; correct notion, right conception, true knowledge, knowledge or information established by proof.
adj. & s.m. Liberating, setting free; — liberator.
s.m. Liberating, setting free, releasing, discharging.
s.f. A species of cucumber.
s.m. Excessive joy, delight, pleasure, gladness, hilarity, happiness.
adj. & s.m. Causing excessive joy, delighting, making happy, gladdening; — a happy person; one who occasions delight; one who makes others happy.
adj. & s.m. Causing excessive joy, delighting, making happy, gladdening; — a happy person; one who occasions delight; one who makes others happy.
part. Delighted, rejoiced, pleased, happy.
s.m. Bewilderment, confusion; infatuation, fascination; insensibility, stupefaction, stupor, fainting away.
adj. = pra-mudit, q.v. part. adj. Delighted, overjoyed, very pleased, happy, glad, content.
part. adj. Delighted, overjoyed, very pleased, happy, glad, content.
foremost, chief, principal
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Facing, first, foremost, chief, principal, most excellent, best; — a chief, a distinguished person, respectable man; a sage; a tree used in dyeing, Rottleria tinctoria.
s.f. = S پرمکهتو प्रमुखत्व pra-mukhatva, s.m. Superiority, predominance; leadership.
s.m. = S پرمکهتا प्रमुखता pra-mukhatā, s.f. Superiority, predominance; leadership.
s.m. Promise, agreement, stipulation, contract; vow, resolution
more: s. m. Promise, agreement, vow, resolution.
subs: paran karnā, v.n. To make a promise; to take a vow; to resolve
s.m. One who gives his pledge, &c.

def: s.f. 'Dear as life,' beloved object, sweetheart, mistress.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀭𑀸𑀡 (purāṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit पुराण (purāṇa).

def: s.m. Breathing, breath, respiration, inspiration, expiration, vital breath, vital action, or life; spirit, soul, vitality; a vital organ, organ of sense, vital air; air inhaled, wind; — anyone as dear as the breath of life, any beloved object, sweetheart, mistress; — poetical talent, inspiration; an epithet of Brahmā the Supreme Spirit; (in Gram.) aspiration in the articulation of letters
subs: (˚ṇa+ap˚), adj. Taking away life, fatal, deadly
(˚ṇa+adh˚), adj. 'More than life,' dearer than life; superior in vigour
(˚ṇa+an˚), s.m. The end of life, death
adj. Destructive to life, fatal, mortal, capital (as (punishment); dangerous, lasting to the end of life, ending with life; — s.m. Murder, assassination
(˚ṇa+ay˚), s.m. Restraining or suspending the breath, or breathing in a peculiar way through the nostrils, during the mental recitation of the names and attributes of some deity
s.m. Danger to life, fear for life, extreme peril
s.m. 'Lord of life,' the soul, heart; a husband
s.f. 'The imparting life' (to an idol), the ceremony of consecrating an idol
s.f. A species of medicinal plant
prāṇ-pyārā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dear as life, dear to the soul, dear, beloved; a lover, sweetheart, darling
v.n. To abandon or yield up life, to give up the ghost; to lose courage, or spirit, or heart
s.m. Abandonment of life, expiring, death, suicide; — prāṇ-tyāg karnā, v.n. To yield up life; to commit suicide
(-kī), To set the soul free, to kill; to save the life (of); to escape with life
v.n. The breath to flit, or to leave (the body), to die; — to be tired, to be out of breath, to be fatigued, or exhausted
s.m. A life-giver, one who saves another's life
s.m. The gift of life; saving anyone's life; resigning or laying down life

s.m. The punishment of death, capital punishment; — prāṇ-danḍ denā (-ko), To pronounce sentence of death; to execute the sentence of death; prāṇ-danḍ-kā bićār karnā (ke), To try (one) for a capital offence; — prāṇ-danḍ khānā, v.n. To suffer capital punishment
s.m. The support or maintenance of life, sustenance, prolongation of life; a means of supporting life; retaining life, vitality
s.m. Any vital part of the body

prāṇ-samān, adj. & s.m. Equal to, or as dear as, life; — one who is as dear as life itself, lover, husband ( = prāṇ-pyārā; prāṇ-tulya); — prāṇ-samā, prāṇ-sumānā, prāṇ-samānī, s.f. 'Equal to life'; a wife
s.m. Checking or suspending the breath (as a religious exercise)
prāṇ-grāhī, adj. & s.m. Seizing or taking life, destroying; — a destroyer of life
adj. Life-destroying, destructive to life, killing
(-kī), 'To take the life' (of); to tease to death, worry, harass, persecute
adj. & s.m. Endowed with breath or life, living, breathing; — a living being; prāṇ-may-kosh, s.m. One of the cases or investitures of the soul, the vital or organic case
s.m. 'Lord of life,' husband; a lover ( = prāṇ-pati); an epithet of Yama
s.m. Destruction or cessation of breath; destruction of the life
adj. Having breath or life, breathing, living, animated; possessing power, strong, powerful
s.f. The science of the breath or vital airs; the science of the soul, psychology
s.m. 'Expenditure of life,' giving up the ghost, death
prāṇ-hārī, prāṇ-har, adj. Taking away life, threatening life, causing death, mortal, deadly, destructive
prāṇeśvar (˚na+īśa, or īśvara), s.m. = prāṇ-pati and prāṇ-nāth, qq.v.
s.f. 'Queen of life,' a wife (an expression of endearment).

def: s.m. Loud sound, clangour, noise, shout, cry; roar; bray; neighing; an exclamation of delight or approbation; a noise or buzzing in the ear (from thickening of the membranes).
s.m. A living being, an animal or sentient being; a species of plant, Celtis orientalis.
s.m. = H پرنالا परनाला parnālā, s.m. = S پرنالي प्रणाली pra-ṇālī, s.f. Channel for water, watercourse, conduit, drain, gutter.
s.f. = S پرنال प्रणाल pra-ṇāl, s.m. = H پرنالا परनाला parnālā, s.m. Channel for water, watercourse, conduit, drain, gutter.
more: s. f. A gutter, a drain, a conduit.

def: s.f. = pra-ṇālī, q.v.; — a tubular vessel of the body. s.f. = S پرنال प्रणाल pra-ṇāl, s.m. = H پرنالا परनाला parnālā, s.m. Channel for water, watercourse, conduit, drain, gutter.
s.m. Bending, bowing; a bow; respectful or reverential salutation, prostration, obeisance (esp. to a Brāhmaṇ or to a deity)
subs: (-ko), To make an obeisance (to), to salute.

def: adj. & s.m. Bending, bowing before, saluting, honouring, worshipping, prostrating oneself; — a worshipper, &c.
s.m. The throat; the act of breathing, respiration; living, life; producing life, calling into life; — adj. Life-producing, giving life to.
s.m. Air, wind; a kind of collyrium.
s.f. Hiccough; sneezing; sobbing.
adj. See s.v. prāṇ. s.m. Promise, agreement, stipulation, contract; vow, resolution
subs: paran karnā, v.n. To make a promise; to take a vow; to resolve
s.m. One who gives his pledge, &c.

def: s.m. Vanishing, disappearance, cessation, loss; death, destruction, perdition, decay.
adj. Causing to disappear or cease, removing, destroying.
part. Bent forwards; bowed, bowing (to), humble, obeisant, courteous, reverential
subs: adj. Nourishing or cherishing the obeisant or the humble.

def: s.f. Bending, bowing, inclination, obeisance, salutation, reverence, courtesy.
s.m. The mystical or sacred syllable Om.
s.m. The mystical or sacred syllable Om.
v.n. To bend or bow down before, make obeisance to, to salute reverentially.

s.m. Confidence, familiarity, trust, intimacy; friendly acquaintance, confidential relationship, fond regard, fondness, affection, love, friendship, favour, kindness; affectionate solicitation, request, asking, begging; reverence, obeisance; final emancipation or beatitude
subs: praṇay-vant, adj. Possessing candour, acting frankly or openly, free from constraint, unceremonious, frank, open; confident; possessing affection, attached (to), feeling an attraction (towards), loving ( = praṇayī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺𑀡𑀻𑀤 (pariṇīda), from Sanskrit परिणीत (pariṇīta, “married”, adjective).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī). Feeling attracted (towards), attached (to), affectionate, loving, kind; liking, wishing or longing (for), desirous (of); beloved, dear; intimate, familiar; confident; affable, kind; — an affectionate friend, favourite; a lover, husband
subs: s.f. A female friend; a mistress, wife.

def: adj. & adv. = prāk, q.v. adj. & adv. Turning eastward, eastern, easterly; preceding in time or place, before, in front, previous, former, prior (in comp., before certain letters, prāk becomes prāg)
subs: s.m. Previous non-existence of anything, the non-existence of anything which may yet be, antecedent privation; non-existence (of an effect) previous (to production); (in Law) the non-possession of property that may be possessed
s.m. Prior or previous existence; superiority, excellence
s.f. The eleventh Nakshatra or lunar mansion
s.m. Name of the country Kāmrūp in Assam (the scene of Brahmā's penance)
s.m. Former age or time, previous time
adj. Belonging to former or ancient times, pertaining to a previous time, anterior, ancient, previous, former
adj. & s.m. Going before, preceding; — a precursor.

def: s.m. A kind of drum ( = pāgan).
s.m. A court, yard, court-yard.
adj. Under a vow; resolute.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺𑀡𑀻𑀤 (pariṇīda), from Sanskrit परिणीत (pariṇīta, “married”, adjective).

def: adj. & s.m. Breathing, living, alive; — a living or sentient being, living creature, animal; a man; — the spirit (of a deceased person), a ghost
more: n. m. 1. A living or sentient being or creature; an animal. 2. The soul ( boltā pursh ); the spirit (of one deceased); ghost.
adj. Having life, an animal, an animated being.
subs: s.f. Pain or cruelty to animals
s.m. (in Gram.) A word indicating an animate object (as distinguished from an inanimate one)

def: s.m. Application, employment, use; access, entrance; respectful conduct (towards), attention, regard; profound religious meditation; great effort, stress, energy; prayer, entreaty, supplication.
adj. Pertaining to life, animal.
s.m. Falling at the feet (of), prostration, humble submission (to); salutation, reverence, obeisance.
s.m. Throwing oneself down at the feet (of anyone), prostration, doing homage (to), saluting.

part. adj. Accomplished, performed, executed, finished, made, done, constructed; prepared, dressed, cooked; written, composed, compiled; thrown, cast, discharged, sent; entered, approached; proper, fit.
s.f. Holding out the open hands slightly hollowed and placed side by side (as if to hold an offering); joining the palms of the hands in a supplicating manner, or as a mark of respect and salutation.
v.t. To make obeisance to, to salute reverentially.
s.m. Edge, margin, border, end, extremity.
s.m. A lonesome way or lane, solitary path; a desolate place, or plain.
adj. & s.m. (used in com.), Causing to attain to; procuring, obtaining; — one who obtains, obtainer, procurer.
s.m. Running away, flight, rout.
adj. & s.m. Running away, flying; — a fugitive, one who deserts his cause.
part. Put to flight, routed, defeated.
s.m. Attaining to, attainment, reaching, extending; obtaining, receiving, acquisition.

s.m. Development, diffusion, display, amplification, expansion; prolixity, diffuseness, copiousness (in style or composition); reverse, opposition, reversion, inversion; analysis; error, illusion, delusion, deceit, trick, fraud.
more: n. m. S. प्रपंच See phar phand . Fraud; cheat; imposture; deceit. Karen parpanch, kahlāëṅ panch . Prov. His deeds unjust, a judge denominate.
s. m. Delusion, fraud, imposition, treachery, deceit, artifice.

def: adj. & s.m. Insidious, artful. fraudulent, deceitful, tricky; — Trickster, deceiver, cneat, rogue, &c.
part. Amplified, expanded, extended, diffused; declared fully, explained, expatiated upon, treated at length; caused to appear in a false light; mistaken, erring; tricked, deceived, beguiled.
s.m. A small herbaceous plant, Hibiscus mutabilis (used as a remedy for ulcers and bad eyes, and as a perfume; vulg. punḍariyā, punḍeriyā).
s.m. The child of a son's son, a great-grandson.
